description: Simulated fan profiles representing different lifecycle stages and segments for Dinamo marketing

lifecycle_stages.awareness: Knows of Dinamo but not engaged
lifecycle_stages.interest: Follows on one platform, occasional engagement
lifecycle_stages.consideration: Actively consuming content, considering first match/purchase
lifecycle_stages.first_action: Attended first match or made first purchase
lifecycle_stages.loyal_fan: Season ticket holder, merchandise buyer, advocate
lifecycle_stages.ambassador: Creates content, recruits other fans, community leader

fan_profiles.name: Marko Novak
fan_profiles.notes: Third-generation Dinamo fan. Father had season ticket. Attends most home matches including European nights.

fan_profiles.name: Ana Jurić
fan_profiles.notes: Became a fan during 2018 World Cup excitement. Mostly engages through social media. Prefers player personality content over tactical.

fan_profiles.name: Ivan Kovačić
fan_profiles.notes: Moved to Munich in 2005 for work. Follows every match online. Returns to Zagreb 2-3 times per season. Buys CL travel packages. Facebook is primary platform.

fan_profiles.name: Luka Tomić
fan_profiles.notes: Lives in Split (Hajduk territory) but started following Dinamo through TikTok content. Not yet converted to attending/purchasing. High potential if content strategy continues.

fan_profiles.name: Maria Horvat-Müller
fan_profiles.notes: Second-generation Croatian-Austrian. Father was a Dinamo fan. Recently started following after seeing a diaspora-targeted Instagram ad. Bilingual but prefers German content.

fan_profiles.name: Tomislav Babić
fan_profiles.notes: 40+ years as a fan. Runs a Dinamo fan blog with 5K followers. Creates content about Dinamo history. Takes his children to matches. Community leader in fan forums.

fan_profiles.name: James Wilson
fan_profiles.notes: Football enthusiast who watches Champions League. Aware of Dinamo through CL coverage. Potential follower if content reaches him through CL-related algorithms. Represents expansion market opportunity.

fan_profiles.name: Petra Šimunović
fan_profiles.notes: From Osijek (has local team) but became Dinamo fan during CL run. Travels to Zagreb for big matches. Very active in social media comments and story replies.

fan_profiles.name: Drago Petrović
fan_profiles.notes: Emigrated to Chicago in 1990. Follows results via Facebook and Croatian news sites. Buys merchandise as gifts. Member of Croatian community in Chicago. Watches CL matches on Paramount+.

fan_profiles.name: Nikola Mandžukić
fan_profiles.notes: Software engineer in Zürich. Watches every match via streaming. Returns for CL home matches. High purchasing power. Would attend more if travel packages were better organized.

fan_profiles.name: Sara Knežević
fan_profiles.notes: Started following Dinamo because school friends talked about TikTok content. Father took her to first match. Plays football in school team. Future academy/fan development target.

fan_profiles.name: Emir Hadžić
fan_profiles.notes: FK Sarajevo fan primarily, but follows Dinamo as regional CL representative. Engages during European matches. Could be converted to regular follower with right content.

fan_profiles.name: Mia Barišić
fan_profiles.notes: Moved to Dublin for work in 2018. Following Dinamo connects her to home. Watches with other Croatians in Dublin pubs. Would buy more if international shipping were cheaper.

fan_profiles.name: Stefan Kolarov
fan_profiles.notes: Slovenian football fan who follows Dinamo in CL as a regional representative. Understands Croatian. Close to attending a CL match in Zagreb (2 hour drive).

fan_profiles.name: David Matić
fan_profiles.notes: Born in Melbourne to Croatian parents. Grew up supporting Dinamo and playing for Melbourne Knights. Watches highlights morning after due to timezone. Active in Croatian-Australian football community.

fan_profiles.name: Antonio Perić
fan_profiles.notes: Runs a Dinamo podcast with 2K listeners per episode. Photographs matches and shares on social media. Organizes fan travel for away European matches. Highest LTV segment.

fan_profiles.name: Lea Vuković
fan_profiles.notes: Second-generation Croatian-German. Discovered Dinamo through TikTok diaspora content. Bought her first Dinamo scarf. Represents key growth demographic - young diaspora re-connecting.

fan_profiles.name: Josip Brkić
fan_profiles.notes: From Varaždin, travels to Zagreb for matches. Only on Facebook. Gets news from Sportske Novosti and 24sata. Represents older domestic fan who needs different content approach.

fan_profiles.name: Filip Petrović
fan_profiles.notes: Data analyst by profession. Loves statistical content and tactical breakdowns. Creates his own analysis threads on Twitter. Bilingual content consumer. Represents growing analytics-fan segment.

fan_profiles.name: Emma Galić
fan_profiles.notes: Swedish-Croatian heritage. Partner is a Dinamo fan and got her interested. Starting to follow on Instagram. Represents 'indirect acquisition' through existing fans. Prefers content with personal/human stories.

fan_profiles.name: Ante Miličević
fan_profiles.notes: Croatian from Herzegovina. Identifies with Dinamo as 'his' club despite distance. Travels to Zagreb for one big match per year. Very active on social media. Represents Bosnian-Croat market segment.

fan_profiles.name: Michael Horvat
fan_profiles.notes: Third-generation Croatian-American. Grandparents emigrated from Zagreb. Active in Croatian Fraternal Union. Visits Croatia occasionally. Would connect with Dinamo through heritage/identity content. Doesn't speak Croatian but has cultural interest.

key_insights: TikTok is primary discovery platform for fans under 25
Facebook dominates 45+ and diaspora in US/AU
Instagram is universal across all segments
Diaspora fans have high emotional connection but low match attendance
Second-generation diaspora needs bilingual content strategy
CL campaigns are the biggest fan acquisition driver
Female fans index higher on behind-scenes and community content
Older fans have highest LTV but lowest digital engagement
Analytics/data content is a growing niche segment
